items.title: Aliyah Boston’s masterful performance leads Fever to 34-point rout of Aces
items.author: Fieldhouse Files
items.body_excerpt: Aliyah Boston met the moment and had a special game in Las Vegas. LAS VEGAS — Aliyah Boston won the tip, opened the game with a floater, and delivered a masterful performance as the Indiana Fever captured a 109-75 wire-to-wire win, their second in eight days against the 2025…

items.title: Thoughts on a Sunday 11:11pm night:
items.author: Leo S
items.body_excerpt:
Thoughts on a Sunday 11:11pm night:

I think Tim Connelly realizes he has to chose between TJ Shannon or Josh Green.

items.title: Asked Baba Miller about Keaton Wagler's 3rd quarter:
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
Asked Baba Miller about Keaton Wagler's 3rd quarter:

"It was great. I feel like no one really panicked after last game. It was a rough game for all of us. And just seeing him today play like he's played the whole year has been great. But I feel that we all kind of expected it."

items.title: Asked Baba Miller about what he is focusing on when it comes to his shooting:
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
Asked Baba Miller about what he is focusing on when it comes to his shooting:

"Just consistency. Just kind of the flow... I got pretty good touch. It's just kind of finding that rhythm where I can have consistent mechanics where it doesn't change from shot to shot and just live with the results"

items.title: Asked Jay Larranaga about what, if anything, he did to help free up Keaton Wagler
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
Asked Jay Larranaga about what, if anything, he did to help free up Keaton Wagler

Jay did not see it that way. He shared that he shared a text from his wife with the team after Thursday's game that said "stay deaf to the negativity and focus on the goal"

items.title: That’s it for me from Vegas. Time for a breather in a cooler climate.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt: That’s it for me from Vegas. Time for a breather in a cooler climate.

items.title: Asked Jay Larranaga how he’s seen Keaton Wagler’s ability to process information: “His processing, his skill level, resiliency, his just really—he plays the game without an agenda. If someone’s open, he passes to them. If he has a mismatch, he attacks it. It’s really fun to watch
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Asked Jay Larranaga how he’s seen Keaton Wagler’s ability to process information: “His processing, his skill level, resiliency, his just really—he plays the game without an agenda. If someone’s open, he passes to them. If he has a mismatch, he attacks it. It’s really fun to watch.”

items.title: LAS VEGAS — Boston set the tone, Mitchell carried the scoring load, Cunningham caught fire from deep, and the Fever delivered their most complete performance of the season.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
LAS VEGAS — Boston set the tone, Mitchell carried the scoring load, Cunningham caught fire from deep, and the Fever delivered their most complete performance of the season.

A 34-point rout to finish a 3-1 road trip and open the second half of the season

www.fieldhousefiles.com/p/fever-aces...

media.title: This is the way: Fever put it all together in 34-point rout of Aces
media.description: Boston delivered 19 points and 11 rebounds, Kelsey Mitchell scored 27, and Sophie Cunningham sank six 3s as Indiana completed a 3-1 road trip with its most complete performance of the season.

items.title: Asked Keaton Wagler how he’s become so adept at processing: “Just trying to stay under control and take all the information in. Play freely is the biggest thing. ... Today we wanted to play fast, and that’s what I did from the start was just try to get the ball out fast and push
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Asked Keaton Wagler how he’s become so adept at processing: “Just trying to stay under control and take all the information in. Play freely is the biggest thing. ... Today we wanted to play fast, and that’s what I did from the start was just try to get the ball out fast and push in transition.”

items.title: Keaton Wagler postgame says Baba Miller is an underrated player. Plays really good defense and doesn't force anything on offense. Does the little things.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Keaton Wagler postgame says Baba Miller is an underrated player. Plays really good defense and doesn't force anything on offense. Does the little things. 

On his own 23-point game: Stayed under control. Learned from last game to handle the physicality and play at his own pace. Playing freely.

items.title: The LA Clippers are 1-1 in Summer League
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
The LA Clippers are 1-1 in Summer League

Final score: Clippers 104, Jazz 82

Nick Martinelli: 14 points, 7/11 FGs, 6 rebounds, 1 steal

FGs: LAC 35/81, 43.%; UTA 27/63, 42.9%

Big possession battle game for Clippers: 15-8 offensive rebound advantage, 14-11 opp. turnover advantage
